key_insight: Larger GPT-style language models delay plasticity loss, but scale alone does not eliminate the eventual inability to adapt to new data.
engineering_relevance: For continual-learning and long-horizon training pipelines, model size can buy time, but robust adaptation likely still requires methods beyond parameter scaling.
method: Trained GPT-style Transformer LLMs on a multilingual continual-learning setup.
Compared models from 5M to 314M non-embedding parameters.
Evaluated plasticity loss using performance deterioration on a held-out Vietnamese probing task.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports measurable plasticity loss across all tested model sizes, with the onset following a sublinear scaling law and also appearing under stationary multilingual training.
watch_item: Results are based on one multilingual probing setup, so the observed scaling behavior may not generalize to all domains, tasks, or mitigation strategies.
paper_title: Can Scale Save Us From Plasticity Loss in Large Language Models?
